# Stop Sign Cameras Coming to Virginia Roads This July

**TL;DR:** Virginia is allowing municipalities to install stop sign cameras starting July to increase traffic safety, though privacy advocates have raised concerns.

A new Virginia law permits local governments to deploy automated cameras at stop signs beginning July 2026. Officials say the cameras will catch drivers running red lights and stop signs while increasing traffic safety and reducing violations. The move has drawn scrutiny from privacy advocates concerned about surveillance expansion.

**Why it matters:** Chesterfield County roads may soon have stop sign cameras, meaning drivers could receive citations for traffic violations captured by automated systems rather than in-person enforcement.

[Read the source at Google News: Virginia Government →](https://news.google.?oc=5)
